properties.remark: SB HIGHWAY 101 IN THE VICINITY OF SOLIMAR BURN AREA CLOSED DUE TO BLOCKED DRAINAGE. WATER AND DEBRIS ON HIGHWAY.

A SMALL TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN RURAL VENTURA COUNTY NEAR THE UNINCORPORATED AREA OF BARDSDALE, CA. DAMAGE WAS NOT CONTINUOUS AND CONFINED MAINLY TO TREES AND FENCES. A STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT AN EF0 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 65-85 MPH HAD OCCURRED.

A U.S. FOREST SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED FLOODING ON LOCKWOOD VALLEY ROAD AT MILE MARKERS 2, 5, 11, AND 12. THERE WAS ABOUT 1 FOOT OF WATER ON THE ROAD WITH MINOR DEBRIS. THE FLOODING WAS OBSERVED AT 10 AM. RAIN GAUGES IN THE AREA REPORTED ABOUT 0.50-0.75 INCHES OF RAIN OVER A PERIOD OF SEVERAL HOURS PRIOR TO THE REPORT OF FLOODING.
FLOODING OF LANDS ON INTERSTATE 710 AT INTERSTATE 5 CONNECTOR. 2 CARS AND 1 TRACTOR TRAILER STUCK IN WATER. CHP REPORT.
MULTIPLE CARS STUCK IN WATER ON 110 FREEWAY NEAR MARISOL BRIDGE. FREEWAY SHUT DOWN. CHP REPORT.
IN 30 MINUTES TIME, 6 INCHES OF MUD FORMED ON BROOKRIDGE AND MELCANYON ROADS IN DUARTE, DOWNSTREAM OF THE FISH BURN AREA. KABC NEWS REPORT.
MANY CARS STUCK IN FLOODED ROAD ON LA CIENAGA NEAR CENTINELA. ROADWAY CLOSED. CHP REPORT.

MUD AND DEBRIS FLOW CAME ACROSS SAND CANYON ROAD NEAR THE SAND BURN AREA. A BULLDOZER WAS REQUIRED TO REMOVE THE MUD AND DEBRIS FOLLOWING THE INCIDENT. CAMP 9 RAWS RECORDED 2.21 INCHES OF RAINFALL IN THE PRECEDING 12 HOURS LEADING UP TO THE EVENT.
MUD AND DEBRIS FLOW DOWN MEL CYN NEAR DEERLANE DR. IN DUARTE BELOW FISH BURN AREA.
